description: The figure displays a logarithmic strain hardening rate plot (ln(dσ/dε) versus ln(σ)) for Fe-12Mn-8Al-0.8C steel, revealing three distinct stages of strain hardening behavior demarcated by critical strains ε₁ = 0.05 and ε₂ = 0.30. The curve spans ln(dσ/dε) values from approximately 6.5 to 8.5 MPa (y-axis) against ln(σ) from 6.5 to 7.2 MPa (x-axis), with Stage 1 showing a sharp decrease, Stage 2 presenting an approximately linear trend, and Stage 3 exhibiting a rapid drop in strain hardening rate. According to the caption and associated citation text, this multi-stage response in the austenite-bainite dual-phase (A-BDP) low-density steel arises from the hardness difference between ferrite and austenite phases: initial single ferrite deformation dominates Stage 1, activation of austenite deformation characterizes Stage 2, and severe strain localization due to mechanical incompatibility between the soft ferrite and hard austenite phases causes the Stage 3 collapse.
